content:
./scripts/rust_is_available.sh: 21: ./scripts/rust_is_available.sh: arithmetic expression: expecting primary: "100000 * + 100 * + "
Makefile:1825: recipe for target 'rustavailable' failed
make: *** [rustavailable] Error 2
anyone meet this error?
Yes, I am sending an improved script very soon for that. Would you be willing to give the patches a test?

I encounter the same error on my side. In my case, bindgen failed to use the libclang provided. But for me, the problem was an incompatibility between my host and the cross compiler toolchain I was trying to use.
@Caozhi you can try to run bindgen ./scripts/rust_is_available_bindgen_libclang.h to see if the failure is because bindgen cannot use libclang. It should provide more information.

@Miguel Ojeda I can easily reproduce the problem on my side, so when you have a patch I can test it. Just ping/CC me.

Razvan Heghedus said:

I encounter the same error on my side. In my case, bindgen failed to use the libclang provided. But for me, the problem was an incompatibility between my host and the cross compiler toolchain I was trying to use.
Caozhi you can try to run bindgen ./scripts/rust_is_available_bindgen_libclang.h to see if the failure is because bindgen cannot use libclang. It should provide more information.

Miguel Ojeda I can easily reproduce the problem on my side, so when you have a patch I can test it. Just ping/CC me.

Thanks! I tried this and got "./scripts/rust_is_available_bindgen_libclang.h:2:9: warning: clang version 17.0.0 (++20230614091518+1e41a29d739a-1~exp1~20230614091627.1303) [-W#pragma-messages], err: false";
there are three versions of libclang in my env, how and which should i set and use?

It seems that your problem might be different than mine. In your case the error is not at the libclang check.
For LLVM 16 or newer you will need to upgrade bingen. See this patch

You can change the libclang with LIBCLANG_PATH env. See https://github.com/KyleMayes/clang-sys#environment-variables
But it should match the clang version used in building the kernel.

You could try and do some hacking around scripts/rust_is_available.sh to put some prints and check again with make LLVM=1 rustavailable to see exactly where it fails.
Thanks！ i add 'LIBCLANG_PATH' and it does work! How can i upgrade bindgen with the patch? it`s the first time to use patch for me.

You could download the patch file with wget/curl and then use git am/apply. You need the raw format. In this case it would be:
curl https://lore.kernel.org/rust-for-linux/20230612194311.24826-1-aakashsensharma@gmail.com/raw | git am
Just a head's up, that this patch seems to apply cleanly only on the rust-for-linux/rust-fixes branch.
Branch at https://github.com/ojeda/linux/tree/rust-is_available, in case that is more convenient for you.
@Caozhi @Razvan Heghedus If you could please give it a go and see if the patch series fixes the issue for you (or otherwise prints a better error for your case), that would be great!

How can i upgrade bindgen with the patch? it`s the first time to use patch for me.

git am works well; however, these days, there is b4 which can download and apply patch series automatically (it also finds new versions, and does a bunch of other stuff). See https://b4.docs.kernel.org/en/latest/index.html, in particular, the shazam and am subcommands. Hope that helps!

@Razvan Heghedus Thanks for giving it a try and reporting your results in the mailing list! I am glad it improved things for you.

By the way, do you want Masahiro/me to convert your message into a Tested-by tag in the commit message? Or perhaps you can send it explicitly to the mailing list if possible, which is ideal for tools like b4.

I'm still learning how to use the mailing list. From my side is fine if the tag is applied by the maintainer, but I guess that's additional work. From what I notice should I just reply to all patches in the series and add Tested-by tag?

I guess that's additional work

It is part of a maintainer's job, so they are expected to do it, no worries :)

In fact, if you give the explicit Tested-by: ... <...> in your email, then b4 will automatically apply the tag to the commit message, and thus is less work for them than having to decide manually and/or having to ask the tagger if it is unclear if they meant to give the tag or not (that is why b4 is quite nice for maintainers).

From what I notice should I just reply to all patches in the series and add Tested-by tag?

Yeah, ideally you would send it for each one that you tested.

Sometimes tags are given in the cover letter (e.g. Reviewed-by), but for Tested-by in a patch series that fix/improve a few orthogonal issues, it can be ambiguous, so it is best to give it explicitly for each patch that you tested.
@Razvan Heghedus @Miguel Ojeda Thank you！ :heart:
